privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic emergency supply rotation task with 12 supply items in a LibreOffice Calc spreadsheet. Verified facts: timeout is 180 seconds (3 minutes), max 15 agent steps, medium difficulty. The task uses realistic shelf life data: bottled water 2 years, canned goods 3 years, alkaline batteries 5 years, primary lithium batteries 10 years, first aid ointments 2 years, bandages 5 years, emergency food bars 5 years. Critical finding: The test data for D-cell lithium batteries contains a discrepancy - the task claims 10-year (3650-day) shelf life but the actual test data only implements 2650 days of shelf life, a 1000-day (approximately 2.7 year) shortfall. Two items (Bottled Water second case and Adhesive Bandages) have missing expiration dates that must be calculated. Status categorization uses thresholds: EXPIRED for days<0, IMMEDIATE for 0-30 days, SOON for 31-90 days, OK for >90 days. All data is synthetic, generated by setup script based on relative dates from today.
